sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: In May 2026, the pharmaceutical landscape achieved a historic milestone with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Arvinas’ Veppanu (vepdegestrant), the first proteolysis-targeting chimera (PROTAC) therapy to reach the market. Approved for metastatic, estrogen receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer with ESR1 mutations, Veppanu functions as an estrogen receptor degrader, demonstrating superior efficacy in the Phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial with a median progression-free survival of 5 months compared to 2.1 months for fulvestrant. This regulatory success validates the clinical viability of targeted protein degradation beyond oncology, as evidenced by concurrent Phase 1 data for ARV-102 in Parkinson’s disease, which achieved over 50% LRRK2 degradation in cerebrospinal fluid. While Arvinas and Pfizer navigate commercialization strategies, including seeking third-party licensing partners for Veppanu, the approval establishes a critical precedent for protein degradation modalities addressing previously undruggable targets. The prescribing information mandates a 200 mg daily dosage and includes warnings for QTc prolongation, supported by the FDA-approved Guardant360 CDx companion diagnostic for patient selection. These developments collectively reinforce confidence in the PROTAC platform’s versatility across oncology and neurodegenerative indications, signaling a maturing therapeutic modality capable of delivering meaningful clinical impact for patients with limited treatment options.

points.text: Arvinas — Veppanu (vepdegestrant) — ESR1m, ER+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer — FDA Approval (May 1, 2026)
Arvinas — ARV-102 — Parkinson’s Disease — Phase 1 Data (50%+ LRRK2 degradation in CSF)
Arvinas: Clinical-stage biotechnology pioneering PROTAC protein degrader platform for oncology and neurodegenerative diseases.
Pfizer: Co-developer of Veppanu, seeking to license commercial rights to third party.
Veppanu: PROTAC estrogen receptor degrader, Approved, ESR1 mutation subset, second-line treatment, Median PFS 5 months vs 2.1 months (fulvestrant), ORR 19% vs 4%.
ARV-102: PROTAC LRRK2 degrader, Phase 1, Parkinson’s patients, 50%+ LRRK2 reduction in CSF, reduced biomarkers (CD68, GPNMB).
Arvinas and Pfizer: Longstanding partnership, currently seeking third-party license for Veppanu commercialization.
